preamble: The hardening and fronting of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into the voiced velar stop [g] is a documented process in several language families, often occurring as part of systemic shifts in the posterior consonant space.

examples.note: Proposed fronting of uvular fricatives to velar stops/fricatives in branches like Lycian (Kloekhorst, 2018).

sources: Kloekhorst, A. (2018). Anatolian evidence suggests that the Indo-European laryngeals *h2 and *h3 were uvular stops. Indo-European Linguistics, 6(1), 69-94.
